title: NERV Ranks
wikitext:
[]: NERV Ranks             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Oct 27, 2006 2:58 pm Would someone explain to me what ranking scale does NERV use for it's personnel? It's almost on par with the Army and the Air Force since the Greek Chorus (Aoba, Ibuki, and Hyuga) are First and Second Lieutenants and Misato was promoted from Captain to Major ...BUT...there is no rank for Commander on that scale. What's up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 27, 2006 4:41 pm Wasn't Fuyutsuki a Commander?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:01 pm Vice Commander

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:02 pm Was Gendo a Commander? that seems kinda wrong to me...

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:06 pm Commander in this sense of the word is the general overseer of the project. So it is correct. Commander Vice Commander Major Captain Lieutenants

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:07 pm cat42 wrote: Was Gendo a Commander? that seems kinda wrong to me...

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:09 pm Admiral Gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:12 pm Gendo = NERV Supreme Commander Fuyutsuki = NERV Vice Commander

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Oct 27, 2006 7:50 pm I recall Misato being promoted from Captain to Major without anyone filling in the space left. Is that how military command works? One free space, just like a slide block puzzle? (I really don't know. Unless I just forgot.)

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 27, 2006 8:00 pm In most cases no but she was still the the Operations Director. If she had been moved into another department someone would have gotten that job and probably the rank of Captain.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sat Oct 28, 2006 3:12 am youd think itd be Navy since Anno is such a huge warship fan...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 28, 2006 5:26 am It seems to be a mixture of military service ranking systems. In Star Trek the Commander is generally the second in command. However in NGE it seems to be in the context of a General or Admiral thus it's hard to pin down just what the hell Anno did.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Oct 28, 2006 5:27 pm keep in mind, Rank isn't the same as "job." Also, the ranking system doesn't really have to conform to any standards whatsoever, especially American ones. It's an anime, and the creators can write it however they damn well please. Also, this is set in Japan, not the US. As for the translations, i suppose they wrote whatever seemed natural.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Mon Oct 30, 2006 12:37 am are rankings different in other countries?... maybe the Nerv ranks match up with a part of the Japanese military

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Oct 30, 2006 5:05 pm Yes rankings are different in other countries, more in their names than their nomenclature though, but that really doesnâ€™t have anything to do with the Eva ranking system. Read the post above yours as Slothen is probably closest to the truth. They just made it up for the series with whatever they wanted and sounded cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 30, 2006 6:18 pm That sounds so Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Thu Nov 23, 2006 12:27 am slothen wrote: keep in mind, Rank isn't the same as "job." Also, the ranking system doesn't really have to conform to any standards whatsoever, especially American ones. It's an anime, and the creators can write it however they damn well please. Also, this is set in Japan, not the US. As for the translations, i suppose they wrote whatever seemed natural.
